card.intuition: A vulnerability where an attacker injects malicious client-side scripts (usually JavaScript) into a web page viewed by other users.
card.analogy: A malicious sticky note: an attacker leaves a note on a public bulletin board that says 'Hey, look at this!'. When you look at it, the note secretly sprays invisible ink (script) on your glasses that lets the attacker see everything you see.
when_to_use: Preventing attackers from stealing user session cookies
Securing user-generated content (comments, profiles, forum posts)
gotchas: Stored XSS is the most dangerous because the script is saved in your database and served to every user who visits that page
Escaping HTML (converting < to <) is the primary defense, but you must choose the right escaping for the right context (HTML, JS, or CSS)

steps.label: Stored
steps.annotation: Stored XSS: injected script saved in DB and served to other users.

steps.label: Reflected
steps.annotation: Reflected XSS: script injected via URL query string — phishing link.

steps.label: Executes
steps.annotation: Victim loads page — script runs in their browser context.

steps.label: Prevent
steps.annotation: Fix: HTML-encode output, use CSP headers, avoid innerHTML with user data.

exercise.prompt: Identify the HTTP header that can prevent XSS by telling the browser which sources of scripts are allowed.
hints: It manages the 'Content' and the 'Policy' of the site
exercise.test_code:
assert "Content" in list(get_security_headers().keys())[0]
assert "Policy" in list(get_security_headers().keys())[0]
print("PASS")
